title: [LLVM][InstCombine] Enable constant folding for SVE asr,lsl and lsr intrinsics.
summary: The generalization extends a pattern-matching optimization into a reusable simplification by mapping SVE intrinsics to their equivalent IR opcodes (e.g., `aarch64_sve_lsl_u` → `Shl`), enabling standard `InstCombine` simplifications to apply. The key insight is that SVE intrinsics guarantee defined results across all lanes, unlike IR instructions which may produce poison/undef for invalid operations (e.g., overshifting). The patch adds `setMatchingIROpcode` for shift/bitwise ops, then in `simplifySVEIntrinsicBinOp`, it guards against simplifications that yield `UndefValue`, preserving correctness. This transforms a single-case fix (constant folding for `mul_u`) into a systematic rule: leverage existing IR folds safely by filtering out undefined results. Developers can apply this by: 1) mapping intrinsics to IR opcodes, 2) reusing generic simplifiers, and 3) adding a safety check for undef/poison.
